what_it_does: Records a posted note by tim in the dragnet-development channel that states a design point about MSG2 gesture semantics: keep tap-to-select as the primary action and reserve long-press for a secondary, more granular command bar including multi-select.
observations: Comment link to board://item-89d62e5d via kind 'commented_on'.
Authored by tim in channel dragnet-development with state posted.
Proposes tap selects at the document's natural block level.
Proposes long-press opens a power menu / right-click-style command bar with actions such as drill finer, multi-select, change level, attach.
Notes this approach avoids scroll conflict for the common tap case.
Ends with an invitation for alternative ideas.
standouts: The explicit split between primary tap selection and expert long-press power menu.
Use of desktop convention as justification for the gesture model.
Positioning long-press as the affordance for granular/secondary actions including multi-select.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a [point] marker to label the note as a design point.
References another noticeboard item with a commented_on link.
notes: Tim's posted comment on item-89d62e5d arguing for tap-to-select and long-press-as-power-menu gesture semantics for MSG2, while remaining open to alternatives.
questions: What is MSG2 and what are its current gesture semantics?
What does 'natural block level' mean in this context?
What is the 'scroll war' being referenced?
What do 'change level' and 'attach' actions refer to?
purpose_vs_actual: The title 'Tim · point' and [point] marker frame the file as a design point; the body records exactly that point, so purpose and content align.
intention_signals: To advocate for a gesture model where tap handles common selection and long-press exposes advanced actions, and to leave room for competing proposals.
novelty: Frames long-press as a desktop-style right-click power menu rather than a selection or drag gesture, explicitly to separate common selection from expert actions and reduce scroll conflict.
contribution: A concrete gesture-semantics proposal and rationale for MSG2, including the tap/long-press split, the desktop-convention analogy, scroll-conflict mitigation, and an explicit openness to alternatives.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by tim commenting on item-89d62e5d, proposing that MSG2 retain tap-to-select as the primary gesture and reserve long-press for a secondary command bar with multi-select and other granular actions.
intention_for_embedding: To record and share a preferred gesture-design stance and invite alternative ideas.
